client_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.h(3)

NAME

client_like.h - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_client_like_config

Typedefs


typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t
typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)
typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Enumerations


enum session_client_like_kind { SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY }

Functions


terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

Provides a unified interface for running client-like modes that share common initialization patterns: media source selection, FPS probing, audio initialization, display creation, splash screen management, keepawake handling, and cleanup.

Each mode registers mode-specific callbacks to handle initialization, the main render loop, and keyboard input. The shared layer handles all the boilerplate.

Modes Supported

  • Mirror mode: Local webcam/media playback without networking
  • Client mode: Network client with per-connection initialization
  • Discovery mode: P2P mode with role negotiation

Shared Responsibilities

This layer automatically handles:

  • Terminal output management (stdout piping detection)
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Splash screen lifecycle and animation
  • Media source selection and FPS probing
  • Audio initialization and lifecycle
  • Display context creation and management
  • Render loop execution
  • Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide:

  • Main loop callback (run_fn) that calls session_render_loop() or custom loop
  • Optional network interrupt callback for socket shutdown
  • Optional custom exit condition for additional shutdown criteria
  • Keyboard handler for interactive controls
  • Terminal behavior preferences (newline on exit)

Memory and Lifecycle

All allocations (capture, display, audio) are owned and cleaned up by session_client_like_run(). Mode callbacks receive initialized, ready-to-use contexts and should not attempt to free them.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file client_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)

Mode-specific main loop callback

Called after all shared initialization is complete (capture opened, audio started, display ready, splash done). The mode runs its main loop here and returns when finished. The shared teardown runs after this returns.

Typical pattern:

// For mirror/discovery participant:
return session_render_loop(capture, display, exit_check_fn, NULL, NULL,


                           keyboard_handler, user_data);
// For client mode (protocol-driven):
while (!should_exit()) {


  // protocol loop...
}
return ASCIICHAT_OK;

Parameters

capture Initialized capture context. Never NULL.
display Initialized display context. Never NULL.
user_data User data passed through from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 98 of file client_like.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum session_client_like_kind

Enumerator

SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY

Definition at line 63 of file client_like.h.

63                                       {
64   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0,
65   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
66   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
67 } session_client_like_kind_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const
  session_client_like_config_t * config)


Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and
    teardown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like
    modes:








Initialization
  (automatic from GET_OPTION())


Terminal:


    
  
  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
    • 
  
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
    • 
  
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility
    • 



Keepawake:


    
  
  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
    • 
  
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
    • 
  
  • Disables keepawake on exit
    • 



Splash Screen:


    
  
  • Creates temporary display context
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
    • 
  
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready
    • 



Media / Webcam
    (session_capture_ctx_t):



  
•

  
Selects source type from options (priority order):





  
 1.

  
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)

  
 2.

  
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" →
      MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN

  
 3.

  
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST

  
 4.

  
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM




    
  
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with
      temporary media source
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
    • 
  
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
    • 
  
  • Destroys capture on exit
    • 



Audio (audio_context_t):


    
  
  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode &&
      snapshot_delay == 0)
    • 
  
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's
    source
    • 
  
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
    • 
  
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
    • 
  
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
    • 
  
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
    • 
  
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
    • 
  
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit
    • 



Display (session_display_ctx_t):


    
  
  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color
    settings
    • 
  
  • Passes audio context if initialized
    • 
  
  • Destroys display on exit
    • 







Mode-Specific
  Loop


Calls config->run_fn(capture, display,
    config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where
    each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own
    protocol-thread-driven loop.








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)


Cleanup sequence (order is critical):



  
1.

  
Re-enable terminal logging

  
2.

  
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources
      FIRST

  
3.

  
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE

  
4.

  
session_display_destroy()

  
5.

  
session_capture_destroy()

  
6.

  
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns

  
7.

  
session_log_buffer_destroy()

  
8.

  
platform_disable_keepawake()

  
9.

  
Print '
    

  




Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration.
  Must not be NULL. run_fn must not be NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 95 of file client_like.c.


95                                                                                       {
96   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
97     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
98   }
99 
100   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
101   g_current_config = config;
102 
103   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
104   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
105 
106   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
107 
108   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
109   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
110   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
111   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
112   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
113   bool audio_available = false;
114 
115   // ============================================================================
116   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
117   // ============================================================================
118 
119   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
120 
121   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
122   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
123   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
124 
125   if (should_force_stderr) {
126     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
127     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
128     log_set_force_stderr(true);
129   }
130 
131   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
132   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
133   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
134   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
135            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
136   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
137       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
138     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
139   }
140 
141   // ============================================================================
142   // SETUP: Keepawake System
143   // ============================================================================
144 
145   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
146 
147   // Validate mutual exclusivity
148   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
149   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
150   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
151 
152   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
153     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
154     goto cleanup;
155   }
156 
157   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
158 
159   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
160   if (!disable_ka) {
161     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
162   }
163 
164   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
165 
166   // ============================================================================
167   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
168   // ============================================================================
169 
170   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
171   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
172   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
173   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
174   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
175   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
176   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
177   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
178   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
179 
180   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
181   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
182   if (!display) {
183     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
184     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
185     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
186     goto cleanup;
187   }
188   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
189 
190   // Register global display context for signal handlers (Ctrl+C help screen closure)
191   // This makes the context available to signal handlers for the entire app lifetime
192   session_display_set_global_context(display);
193 
194   // ============================================================================
195   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
196   // ============================================================================
197 
198   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
199 
200   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
201   splash_intro_start(display);
202   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
203 
204   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
205   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
206   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
207   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
208 
209   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
210   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
211     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
212     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, 250);
213   }
214   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
215 
216   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
217   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
218   splash_restore_stderr();
219 
220   // ============================================================================
221   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
222   // ============================================================================
223 
224   // ============================================================================
225   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
226   // ============================================================================
227 
228   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d, discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
229   // For discovery mode, skip disabling logging initially - we'll handle it after role is determined
230   // Discovery participants should show logs, only hosts should have logs disabled for rendering
231   bool should_disable_logging = !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && (config->discovery == NULL);
232   if (should_disable_logging) {
233     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
234     log_set_terminal_output(false);
235     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
236   } else {
237     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Skipping log disable (snapshot=%d or discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
238   }
239   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging setup complete, starting media source setup");
240 
241   // ============================================================================
242   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
243   // ============================================================================
244 
245   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
246   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
247   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
248   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
249   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
250 
251   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
252   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
253   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
254   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
255   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
256 
257   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
258   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
259   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
260   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
261   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
263             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
264 
265   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
266     // Network URL takes priority
267     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
268     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
269     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
270     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
271 
272     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
273       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
274       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
275     } else {
276       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
277       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
278       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
279       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
280       if (probe_source) {
281         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
282         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
283         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
284           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
285         } else {
286           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
287           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
288         }
289       } else {
290         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
291         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
292       }
293     }
294   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
295     // Local file or stdin
296     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
297     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
298       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
299       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
300       capture_config.path = NULL;
301       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
302       capture_config.loop = false;
303     } else {
304       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
305       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
306       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
307       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
308 
309       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
310         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
311         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
312       } else {
313         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
314         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
315         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
316         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
317         if (probe_source) {
318           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
319           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
320           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
321             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
322           } else {
323             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
324             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
325           }
326         } else {
327           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
328           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
329         }
330       }
331     }
332   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
333     // Test pattern
334     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
335     log_info("Using test pattern");
336     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
337     capture_config.path = NULL;
338     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
339     capture_config.loop = false;
340   } else {
341     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
342     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
343     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
344     if (stdout_is_tty) {
345       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
346       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
347       log_info("Using local webcam");
348       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
349       capture_config.path = NULL;
350       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
351       capture_config.loop = false;
352     } else {
353       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
354       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
355       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
356       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
357       capture_config.path = NULL;
358       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
359       capture_config.loop = false;
360     }
361   }
362 
363   // Apply initial seek if specified
364   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
365   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
366   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
367 
368   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
369   if (probe_source) {
370     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
371     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
372     probe_source = NULL;
373     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
374   } else {
375     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
376   }
377 
378   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
379   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
380     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
381     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
382               capture_config.target_fps);
383   }
384 
385   // ============================================================================
386   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
387   // ============================================================================
388 
389   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
390 
391   bool networked_mode = session_client_like_is_networked(config->kind);
392 
393   switch (config->kind) {
394     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR:
395       log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
396       break;
397     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT:
398       log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
399       break;
400     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY:
401       log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
402       break;
403   }
404 
405   // ============================================================================
406   // SETUP: Capture Context
407   // ============================================================================
408 
409   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
410   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
411   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
412   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
413   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
414   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
415   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
416   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
417   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
418   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
419   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
420                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
421                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
422 
423   // Enable audio capture if render-file is active and media source exists
424   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0 && has_media_source) {
425     capture_config.enable_audio = true;
426     log_debug("Audio capture enabled for render-file encoding");
427   }
428 
429   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
430                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
431                             !has_media_source && config->kind != SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR);
432 
433   if (stdin_render_mode) {
434     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
435     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
436 
437     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
438     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
439 
440     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
441     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
442 
443     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
444       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
445                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
446       goto cleanup;
447     }
448 
449     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
450     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
451       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
452       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
453       goto cleanup;
454     }
455 
456     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
457     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
458     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
459     if (!capture) {
460       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
461       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
462       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
463       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
464       goto cleanup;
465     }
466 
467     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
468   }
469 
470   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
471   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
472   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
473   if (!stdin_render_mode && networked_mode) {
474     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
475     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
476     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
477     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
478     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
479     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
480     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
481     if (!capture) {
482       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
483       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
484       goto cleanup;
485     }
486     if (fps > 0) {
487       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
488     }
489   }
490 
491   // ============================================================================
492   // SETUP: Audio Context
493   // ============================================================================
494 
495   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
496   bool should_init_audio = true;
497   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
498     should_init_audio = false;
499     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
500   }
501 
502   // Probe for audio
503   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
504     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
505     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
506       audio_available = true;
507 
508       // Allocate and initialize audio context
509       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
510       if (audio_ctx) {
511         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
512         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
513           // Link audio to media source
514           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
515           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
516 
517           if (media_source) {
518             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
519           }
520 
521           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
522           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
523           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
524 
525           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
526           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
527             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
528             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
529           }
530 
531           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
532           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
533 
534           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
535         } else {
536           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
537           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
538           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
539           audio_ctx = NULL;
540           audio_available = false;
541         }
542       }
543     }
544   }
545 
546   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
547   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
548     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
549     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
550   }
551 
552   // ============================================================================
553   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
554   // ============================================================================
555   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
556 
557   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
558   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
559   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
560   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
561   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
562   // connection is established.
563 
564   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
565 
566   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
567   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
568     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
569     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
570     splash_intro_done();
571     splash_wait_for_animation();
572     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
573     goto cleanup;
574   }
575 
576   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
577   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
578   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
579   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
580   if (config->discovery == NULL) {
581     splash_intro_done();
582     if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
583       splash_wait_for_animation();
584     }
585   } else {
586     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Discovery mode: keeping splash visible while waiting for peers");
587   }
588 
589   // ============================================================================
590   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
591   // ============================================================================
592 
593   log_info("[MIRROR_CHECK] stdin_render_mode=%d, capture=%p", stdin_render_mode, (void *)capture);
594   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
595     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
596     log_info("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
597     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
598     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
599     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
600     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
601     if (!capture) {
602       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
603       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
604       goto cleanup;
605     }
606 
607     // Set audio source for render-file encoding (after capture is created)
608     media_source_t *render_audio_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
609     const char *render_file_path = GET_OPTION(render_file);
610     log_info("[AUDIO_SOURCE] render_audio_source=%p, render_file=%s, render_file_len=%zu",
611              render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
612              render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
613     if (render_audio_source && render_file_path && strlen(render_file_path) > 0) {
614       session_display_set_render_audio_source(display, render_audio_source);
615       log_info("Audio source set for render-file output");
616     } else {
617       log_warn("[AUDIO_SOURCE] Conditions not met: render_audio_source=%p, render_file_path=%s, len=%zu",
618                render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
619                render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
620     }
621   }
622 
623   // ============================================================================
624   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
625   // ============================================================================
626 
627   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
628   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
629     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
630     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
631       log_info("Audio playback started");
632     } else {
633       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
634       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
635       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
636       audio_ctx = NULL;
637       audio_available = false;
638     }
639   }
640 
641   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
642   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
643 
644   // ============================================================================
645   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
646   // ============================================================================
647 
648   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
649   int attempt = 0;
650   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
651 
652   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
653 
654   while (true) {
655     attempt++;
656 
657     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
658 
659     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
660     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
661       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
662       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
663 
664       break;
665     }
666 
667     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
668     bool should_retry = false;
669 
670     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
671       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
672       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
673         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
674       } else {
675         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
676       }
677 
678       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
679       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
680         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
681         should_retry = false;
682       }
683     }
684 
685     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
686     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
687       break;
688     }
689 
690     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
691     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
692     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
693 
694     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
695     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
696     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
697     splash_intro_start(display);
698 
699     // Log reconnection message
700     if (max_attempts == -1) {
701       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
702     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
703       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
704     }
705 
706     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
707     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
708     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
709       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
710       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
711 
712       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
713         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
714         APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
715         APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, sleep_ms);
716         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
717       }
718     }
719 
720     // Continue loop to retry
721   }
722 
723   // ============================================================================
724   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
725   // ============================================================================
726 
727 cleanup:
728   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
729   log_set_terminal_output(true);
730 
731   g_current_config = NULL;
732   g_render_should_exit = NULL;
733 
734   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
735   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
736   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
737 
738   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
739   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
740   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
741 
742   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
743   if (audio_ctx) {
744     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
745     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
746     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
747     audio_ctx = NULL;
748   }
749 
750   // Destroy display (check global context to see if discovery mode already destroyed it)
751   // Discovery participant mode may destroy the display early and clear the global context
752   session_display_ctx_t *current_global = session_display_get_global_context();
753   if (display && display == current_global) {
754     // Display is still valid (not destroyed by discovery mode)
755     session_display_destroy(display);
756     display = NULL;
757   } else if (display && display != current_global) {
758     // Display was already destroyed (by discovery participant mode)
759     // Just NULL it out, don't call destroy again
760     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Display was already destroyed by discovery mode, skipping destroy");
761     display = NULL;
762   }
763 
764   // Destroy capture
765   if (capture) {
766     session_capture_destroy(capture);
767     capture = NULL;
768   }
769 
770   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
771   if (g_stdin_reader) {
772     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
773     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
774   }
775 
776   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
777   if (probe_source) {
778     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
779     probe_source = NULL;
780   }
781 
782   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
783   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
784   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
785   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
786   if (config->discovery != NULL) {
787     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Discovery mode: keeping splash alive for peer waiting UI");
788   } else if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
789     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
790     splash_intro_done();
791     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
792 
793     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
794     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
795     splash_wait_for_animation();
796     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
797   } else {
798     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
799     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
800     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
801   }
802 
803   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
804   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
805   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
806   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
807 
808   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
809   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
810   platform_disable_keepawake();
811   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
812 
813   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
814   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
815   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
816     log_debug("Writing newline");
817     const char newline = '\n';
818     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
819   }
820 
821   log_set_terminal_output(false);
822 
823   return result;
824 }






References APP_CALLBACK_BOOL, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(), audio_init(),
    audio_should_enable_microphone(), audio_start_duplex(),
    audio_stop_duplex(), audio_stop_thread(),
    audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio, ERROR_DISPLAY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_enabled,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_filled,
    session_client_like_config::kind, log_debug, log_fatal,
    log_info, log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_warn,
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    audio_context_t::media_source, media_source_create(),
    media_source_destroy(), MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST, MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT, OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT,
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(), audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_context_t::playback_only,
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR, session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_get_global_context(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_global_context(),
    session_display_set_render_audio_source(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps, TERM_COLOR_AUTO,
    terminal_fd_reader_create(), terminal_fd_reader_destroy(),
    terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
    terminal_should_force_stderr(), time_elapsed_ns(),
    time_get_ns(), and session_capture_config_t::type.


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().








Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Get the render loop should_exit callback set by
    session_client_like_run().




This is used by mode-specific run_fn callbacks to obtain the
    proper should_exit callback for passing to session_render_loop(). The
    callback checks both the global should_exit() flag and any
    mode-specific custom_should_exit condition.




Returns


Function pointer to the render should_exit callback, or
  NULL if session_client_like_run() has not been called yet.


Definition at line 245 of file client_like.h.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
